Definition: A member of a race of people who were the first people to live in a country, before any colonists arrived.
Source: aboriginal. (2023b, March 8). https://dictionary.cambridge.org/dictionary/english/aboriginal

Definition: A group of people organized for a joint purpose
Source: https://www.lexico.com/definition/association

Definition: Wide array of organizations: community groups, non-governmental organizations [NGOs], labor unions, indigenous groups, charitable organizations, faith-based organizations, professional associations, and foundations.
Source: Who and what is “civil society?” (2021, January 25). World Economic Forum. https://www.weforum.org/agenda/2018/04/what-is-civil-society/

Definition: Society where social and individual ties are strong, with people being part of strong cohesive groups.
Source: Prabhu, V. (2011). Risk-Taking. Encyclopedia of Creativity, 319–323. https://doi.org/10.1016/b978-0-12-375038-9.00189-8

Definition: A language used for communication between groups of people who speak different languages. Also known as Lingua Franca.
Source: lingua franca. (2023). https://dictionary.cambridge.org/es/diccionario/ingles/lingua-franca

Definition: Goal-driven activity in which one is either conveying some information to the other party to get them to do something or are trying to extract information from another person to further one 's own objectives.
Source: Fong, M. (2009). The spirituality of “Being” Grace and Tao in Intercultural Communication. In Samovar, Porter, McDaniel (Eds.) (2009) Intercultural Communication. Wadsworth, pp.145-161.

Definition: Individual or organization’s fundamental beliefs and highest priorities that drive their behavior.
Source: Hailey, L. (2022, September 22). Core Values List: The Only 216 Values You’ll Ever Need. Science of People. https://www.scienceofpeople.com/core-values/

Definition: Pattern of shared beliefs and values which gives a meaning for the members of an institution and provide them with the behavior rules in their organization.
Source: Davis, S. M. (1984). Managing corporate culture. Harvard Business Review, 62(1), 70-80

Definition: is at once an artifact of how strategic thoughts are formulated as well as how they are communicated and discussed. Therefore, the language used by decision makers in a company both shapes and bounds what a firm focuses on and develops its competencies around as well as how it articulates its strategic options.
Source: Brannen, M. Y., & Doz, Y. L. (2012). Corporate Languages and Strategic Agility: Trapped in Your Jargon or Lost in Translation? California Management Review, 54(3), 77–97. https://doi.org/10.1525/cmr.2012.54.3.77

Definition: Culture's core beliefs about what is good or right.
Source: Cultural values - Care Quality Commission. (n.d.). https://www.cqc.org.uk/guidance-providers/adult-social-care/cultural-values

Definition: the set of shared attitudes, values, goals, and practices that characterizes an institution or organization.
Source: Merriam-Webster. (n.d.) Culture definition & meaning. Merriam-Webster. https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/culture

Definition: A language is considered foreign if it is learned largely in the classroom and is not spoken in the society where the teaching occurs.
Source: Moeller, A.K and Catalano, T. (2015). "Foreign Language Teaching and Learning" Faculty Publications: Department of Teaching, Learning and Teacher Education. (p.327)

Definition: A diverse social universe in which the unifying forces of modern production, markets, communications and cultural and political modernisation interact with many global, regional, national and local segmentations and differentiations.
Source: Shaw, M. (1994) The Theoretical Challenge of Global Society in Global Society and International Relations.

Definition: The system by which a state or community is governed
Source: https://www.lexico.com/definition/government [1.1]

Definition: Inheritors and practitioners of unique cultures and ways of relating to people and the environment. They have retained social, cultural, economic and political characteristics that are distinct from those of the dominant societies in which they live.
Source: Indigenous Peoples at the United Nations | United Nations For Indigenous Peoples. (n.d.). https://www.un.org/development/desa/indigenouspeoples/about-us.html

Definition: A single person or thing, especially when compared to the group or set to which they belong
Source: CAMBRIDGE DICTIONARY. (2021). https://dictionary.cambridge.org/es/diccionario/ingles/individual. Retrieved March 9th 2021, https://dictionary.cambridge.org/es/diccionario/ingles/individual

Definition: Exists when a group of like-minded states ‘conceive themselves to be bound by a common set of rules in their relations with one another, and share in the working of common institutions.
Source: Bull, H. (1977). The Anarchical Society: A Study of Order in World Politics. Columbia University Press.

Definition: A system of conventional spoken, manual (signed), or written symbols by means of which human beings, as members of a social group and participants in its culture, express themselves.
Source: Robins, R. H. & Crystal, D. (2020, October 29). Language. Encyclopedia Britannica. https://www.britannica.com/topic/language

Definition: Language as an Individual Characteristic of MNC Employees / is not separate from but rather influenced by the user’s cultural and historical context
Source: Karhunen, P., Kankaanranta, A., Louhiala-Salminen, L., & Piekkari, R. (2018). Let’s Talk about Language: A Review of Language-Sensitive Research in International Management. Journal of Management Studies, 55(6), 980–1013.

Definition: Language as a social activity
Source: Karhunen, P., Kankaanranta, A., Louhiala-Salminen, L., & Piekkari, R. (2018). Let’s Talk about Language: A Review of Language-Sensitive Research in International Management. Journal of Management Studies, 55(6), 980–1013.

Definition: [Language as a] variable to be designed / language is an object that resides outside the user of the language, in grammar books and dictionaries / language as a top management problem
Source: Karhunen, P., Kankaanranta, A., Louhiala-Salminen, L., & Piekkari, R. (2018). Let’s Talk about Language: A Review of Language-Sensitive Research in International Management. Journal of Management Studies, 55(6), 980–1013.

Definition: Group of people who have been brought up within a given country. In comparison to others, these individuals tend to share certain expectations of how things should be done and values around these expectations. The expectations are a product of the environment we grow up in.
Source: Hofstede Insights. (2021, November). National Culture and Organisational Culture - how are they different and how do they interconnect? https://news.hofstede-insights.com/news/national-culture-and-organisational-culture-how-are-they-differen

Definition: Government, or political authority, that controls a nation. At minimum, it requires a national army, enough power over its states or provinces to set and maintain foreign policy, and the ability to collect taxes.
Source: National Government: Definition, Responsibilities & Powers. (2016, March 15). Retrieved from https://study.com/academy/lesson/national-government-definition-responsibilities-powers.html.

Definition: A language learned in childhood in the home environment, also referred to as mother tongue, first language, or home language.
Source: UNESCO. (2022). https://tcg.uis.unesco.org/wp-content/uploads/sites/4/2020/08/Metadata-4.5.2.pdf

Definition: Relating to or describing someone's country or place of birth or someone who was born in a particular country or place.
Source: native. (2023, March 8). https://dictionary.cambridge.org/us/dictionary/english/native

Definition: Political system by which a country or community is administered and regulated.
Source: Encyclopedia Britannica. https://www.britannica.com/topic/government

Definition: How the members of an organization relate to each other, to their work and to the outside world.
Source: Hofstede Insights. (2021, November). National Culture and Organisational Culture - how are they different and how do they interconnect? https://news.hofstede-insights.com/news/national-culture-and-organisational-culture-how-are-they-differen

Definition: Human beings in general or considered collectively
Source: https://www.lexico.com/definition/people [1]

Definition: The set of formal legal institutions that constitute a “government” or a “state”
Source: Heslop, D. Alan (2020, October 30). Political system. Encyclopedia Britannica. https://www.britannica.com/topic/political-system

Definition: Representations that are consumed by a mass audience, often because they are created through a process of mass production. In its most basic form, popular culture simply refers to widely shared images, ideas, and objects - the culture of the people.
Source: Kidd, D., Kim, J., & Turner, A. (2017). Popular Culture. In K. Korgen (Ed.), The Cambridge Handbook of Sociology: Specialty and Interdisciplinary Studies (pp. 284-292). Cambridge: Cambridge University Press. doi:10.1017/9781316418369.03

Definition: It covers norms, expectations, and shared values of a society or a group of people living in a particular place.
Source: Onyusheva, I., Thammashote, L., & Thongaim, J. (2020). Multicultural Urban Business Environment. Advances in Finance, Accounting, and Economics Book Series. https://doi.org/10.4018/978-1-7998-0111-5.ch012

Definition: People group, more or less large and complex, associated for some common interest and characterized by distinctive hierarchical relationships
Source: Zinzan. Retrieved from: IATE. (n. d.). Retrieved March 8th 2021, from https://iate.europa.eu/entry/slideshow/1615311067272/67759/en-en-la-mul

Definition: A voluntary association, sometimes a corporation, organized and existing for the mutual benefit of its members in patriotic, religious, charitable, or professional pursuits or for providing benefits in case of illness or disability of a member or for his widow or children in the event of his death
Source: Black's Law Dict., 6 ed., p. 1390.; Ballentine's Law Dict., 3e éd., p. 1190. Retrieved from: IATE. (n. d.). Retrieved March 8th 2021, from https://iate.europa.eu/entry/slideshow/1615311067272/67759/en-en-la-mul // Nore to 2nd definition

Definition: One that does not exist in reality and, moreover, does not pretend to resemble a real national culture.
Source: Hofstede, G. J., & Pedersen, P. (1999). Synthetic Cultures: Intercultural Learning Through Simulation Games. Simulation & Gaming, 30(4), 415–440. https://doi.org/10.1177/1046878199030004

Definition: Broad tendencies to prefer certain states of affairs over others.
Source: Hofstede, G. and Hofstede, G. J. (2005). Cultures and Organizations: Software of the Mind. McGraw-Hill, New York

Definition: The Earth with all its inhabitants and all things upon it
Source: IATE. (n. d.). https://iate.europa.eu/entry/slideshow/1615311624694/49838/en-en-la-mul. Retrieved March 8th 2021, from https://iate.europa.eu/entry/slideshow/1615311624694/49838/en-en-la-mul.

Definition: World society is associated with a political system in which states are not the predominant actors, although this does not mean they disappear; where political activity is principally focused upon individuals, rather than institutionalized collectives; and where normative progress is understood in universal terms.
Source: Williams, M. C. (2005). The Realist Tradition and the Limits of International Relations. Cambridge University Press, p. 20.
